query: James P.K. Sangma NPP candidate Meghalaya Rajya Sabha nomination June 2026
answer:
## Research Report: James P.K. Sangma — NPP Candidate for Meghalaya Rajya Sabha, June 2026

---

### 1. Who is James P.K. Sangma?

James P.K. Sangma is a senior leader of the **National People's Party (NPP)** and a prominent politician from Meghalaya.

- **Family**: Brother of current Meghalaya Chief Minister **Conrad K. Sangma**; son of the late **Purno Agitok Sangma (P.A. Sangma)**, former Speaker of the Lok Sabha (1996–1998) and founder of the NPP.
- **Current Party Role**: **National Working President of the NPP** since March 2025.
- **Previous Rajya Sabha Tenure**: Served as Rajya Sabha MP from **2008 to 2014**.
- **State-Level Experience**: Meghalaya Cabinet Minister from **2018 to 2021**.
- **Recent Electoral Setback**: Lost his assembly seat in the **2023 Meghalaya state elections**, leaving him without a legislative position.

---

### 2. Rajya Sabha Seat Vacancy and Election Details

- The seat being vacated is held by **Dr. W.R. Kharlukhi** (NPP), elected in June 2020, whose 6-year term expires in 2026.
- **Election Commission of India announced the election on May 22, 2026.**
- **Polling date: June 18, 2026** (9 AM–4 PM; counting at 5 PM same day).
- **Nomination filing deadline: June 8, 2026.**
- **Process completion deadline: June 20, 2026.**
- Meghalaya has **only 1 Rajya Sabha seat** up for election.

---

### 3. Official Nomination Status

**Yes — officially nominated.** The MDA-II (Meghalaya Democratic Alliance-II) ruling coalition announced James P.K. Sangma as their candidate on **June 3, 2026**, at a meeting chaired by CM Conrad K. Sangma. He was expected to **file his nomination papers on June 5, 2026 at 2:30 PM**. He carries the backing of the entire ruling alliance, not merely the NPP.

---

### 4. Competing Candidates and Political Dynamics

- **No opposition candidate had been publicly announced as of June 3, 2026.**
- The MDA-II coalition controls **51 of 60 assembly seats** — an 85% supermajority. Only **31 votes** are needed to win.
- The opposition holds a combined **9 seats** (AITC/TMC: 5, VPP: 4), making any opposition candidacy mathematically futile.
- **Historical precedent**: Single-seat state Rajya Sabha elections in India from 2020–2026 have had a near-100% rate of unopposed outcomes.
- James P.K. Sangma's election is **virtually certain**.

---

### 5. Current Meghalaya Assembly Composition

| Party / Group | Seats |
|---|---|
| **NPP (National People's Party)** | **33** |
| UDP (United Democratic Party) | 12 |
| BJP | 2 |
| HSPDP | 2 |
| Independents (pro-govt) | 2 |
| **Total Ruling Coalition (MDA-II)** | **51** |
| AITC / TMC | 5 |
| VPP (Voice of the People Party) | 4 |
| **Total Opposition** | **9** |
| **Total Assembly** | **60** |

The NPP alone holds 33 seats — more than the 31 needed for a simple majority, meaning the party could elect its candidate without coalition partners.

---

### Summary

James P.K. Sangma, NPP National Working President and brother of CM Conrad Sangma, has been officially nominated by the ruling MDA-II coalition for the Meghalaya Rajya Sabha seat. With 51 of 60 MLAs backing him and no opposition candidate announced, his election on June 18, 2026 is a near-certainty. This would mark his return to the Rajya Sabha after a 12-year gap (previously served 2008–2014). His nomination filing was scheduled for June 5, 2026.
